summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on September 27, 2022. Duke Energy presented a $50,000 check to the Lions Club and a $10,000 check to the Goshen CPAAA for tornado relief efforts, with 48 families assisted to date. The board approved resolutions related to the Ohio opioid settlement, declared a surplus vehicle for sale, approved police training, rescinded a firefighter appointment, authorized a suspension, and designated October 31 as Halloween night. Updates were provided on ongoing tornado recovery including Station 18 repairs, fence repairs at the PD lot, and insurance work.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Sept 14-27, 2022) - passed
Motion to declare surplus and sell 2002 Ford Ranger 4x2 pickup truck on govdeals.com - passed
Motion to approve repurchase of Lot 342, Grave Number 1, Section A at $500 - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town training for Detective McMillan and Officer Varon for advanced interviewing and interrogation techniques ($720 each) - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-052-2022 approving participation in Region 14 governance structure under Ohio One Ohio MOU - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-053-2022 approving initial Region 14 representative to Ohio One Ohio Recovery Foundation Inc. Board - passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:39 PM - passed
Motion to rescind January 30, 2022 contingent appointment of part-time firefighter paramedic Cody Bryan - passed
Motion to authorize two-shift unpaid suspension of a full-time firefighter - passed
Motion to designate October 31, 2022 as Halloween night - passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:51 PM - passed
action_items: Harry to review zoning inspector position applications with the board
Dan to coordinate AC and breathing air system inspections at Station 18 before disconnecting generator and portable AC
Dan to follow up on generator delivery for the tower project
Dan to finalize temporary station setup (phone and cable pending)
Cheryl to continue working on property casualty insurance renewal and health care renewal
Cheryl to coordinate remaining roof and building repairs at administration building
Dan to get the new service department truck completed and in service by mid-October
Bob to follow up on Public Assistance Program reimbursement through Ohio EMA
Board to determine a safe location for Halloween event given tornado damage
key_amounts: $50,000 - Duke Energy donation to Lions Club for tornado relief
$10,000 - Duke Energy donation to Goshen CPAAA for tornado relief
$500 - repurchase price for cemetery Lot 342, Grave 1, Section A
$720 each - cost for Detective McMillan and Officer Varon training
$808 million - Ohio's total opioid settlement amount
notable_topics: Duke Energy tornado relief donations to Lions Club and CPAAA
Ongoing tornado recovery and infrastructure repairs (Station 18, PD fence, roof, tower)
Ohio opioid settlement participation and Region 14 governance
Rescission of part-time firefighter paramedic appointment
Two-shift unpaid suspension of full-time firefighter
Linton-Goshen Road connector preliminary design discussion
Zoning inspector position posted with two applications received
notes: The transcript has significant portions of garbled/corrupted text due to automated transcription issues. The community is still recovering from a tornado that caused substantial damage to township facilities including Station 18, the police department fence, administration building roof, and the communications tower. Duke Energy representatives attended to present ceremonial donation checks. The Lions Club reported assisting 48 families with tornado relief. The CPAAA used its donation to help township workers with vehicle losses and insurance deductibles from the tornado event. There was discussion about difficulty finding a safe location for Halloween activities due to tornado damage.
title: Reg Mtg BPT 9.27.22
